seo.title: Arizona CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Arizona - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity arizona, how to transfer CNA license to arizona, arizona CNA endorsement application

board.name: Arizona State Board of Nursing
board.mailing_address: 1740 W Adams St, Suite 2000, Phoenix, AZ 85007

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Arizona.

steps.title: Complete Approved Training Program
steps.description: Successfully complete a Nursing Assistant Training Program approved by the Arizona Board of Nursing.

steps.title: Pass Competency Examination
steps.description: Register for and pass both the written and skills portions of the Arizona state CNA competency examination, which is administered by Credentia (credentia.org/cna/az).

steps.title: Create AZBN Nurse Portal Account and Apply
steps.description: Create an account on the AZBN Nurse Portal (azbn.glsuite.us) as a new applicant. Log in and select 'Apply for a New License', then choose 'Nursing Assistant by Exam' to start your application.

steps.title: Submit Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Complete the required fingerprint background check process as instructed during the online application on the AZBN Nurse Portal. This involves obtaining fingerprint cards and submitting them for processing.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee and Upload Documents
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $50 and upload all necessary supporting documents, such as your training completion certificate and examination results, through the AZBN Nurse Portal.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Arizona CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $50. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: No, fingerprints are generally not required for CNA endorsement in Arizona.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Arizona?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Arizona while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in AZ.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Arizona.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Arizona
hero.dek: To apply for a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) license in Arizona, you must create an account and complete the application through the AZBN Nurse Portal.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Arizona
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Arizona CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the AZBN Nurse Portal. Click on 'New Applicant'. Enter a valid email address and password, then click 'Create New Account'. Check your email for a verification link to activate your account. Once activated, log in to the portal.
